description: B2B SaaS companies typically ranging from 5-200 employees, selling software subscriptions with deal sizes from $500 to $5K annually. Characterized by metrics-driven decision making, rapid growth focus, and committee-based purchasing involving 6-11 stakeholders for mid-market deals.

pain_points.pain: High customer churn eroding ARR growth
pain_points.context: Net revenue retention below 100% indicates losing more revenue from churned customers than gaining from expansions
Customer acquisition costs taking too long to recover, threatening cash position

pain_points.pain: Difficulty proving ROI within expected timeframe
pain_points.context: 57% of buyers expect to see ROI within 3 months of purchase

pain_points.pain: Security and compliance requirements blocking deals
pain_points.context: Enterprise prospects requiring SOC 2, data residency, and security questionnaires

pain_points.pain: Product-led growth metrics not translating to revenue
pain_points.context: High activation but low conversion from free users to paid customers

pain_points.pain: Competitor lock-in making switching difficult for prospects
pain_points.context: Prospects hesitant to switch due to data migration and integration dependencies

buying_triggers: Funding round with mandate to improve efficiency
New product launch requiring supporting tools
Security audit or SOC 2 certification timeline
AI initiative or automation mandate from leadership
budget_cycle: Quarterly budget reviews with annual planning. Post-funding periods often have accelerated purchasing authority. End of quarter urgency common.
regulatory_concerns: Data residency requirements for enterprise customers

terminology.definition: Annual Recurring Revenue - the annualized value of subscription contracts
terminology.usage_context: Primary growth metric. 'We hit $2M ARR this quarter.'

terminology.definition: Monthly Recurring Revenue - subscription revenue normalized to monthly basis
terminology.usage_context: Used for month-over-month growth tracking. 'MRR grew 8% last month.'

terminology.definition: Net Revenue Retention - revenue retained from existing customers including expansions and contractions
terminology.usage_context: Health metric for customer success. 'Our NRR is 115% meaning customers expand over time.'

terminology.definition: Customer Acquisition Cost - total cost to acquire a new customer
terminology.usage_context: Sales efficiency metric. 'Our CAC payback is under 12 months.'

terminology.definition: Lifetime Value - total revenue expected from a customer over their relationship
terminology.usage_context: Used with CAC for unit economics. 'We need LTV:CAC of at least 3:1.'

terminology.definition: Percentage of customers (not revenue) lost in a period
terminology.usage_context: Customer retention metric. 'Logo churn is 5% annually but revenue churn is only 2%.'

terminology.definition: Net burn divided by net new ARR - measures capital efficiency
terminology.usage_context: Investor efficiency metric. 'Our burn multiple is under 2x.'

terminology.definition: Product-Led Growth - strategy where product usage drives acquisition and expansion
terminology.usage_context: Go-to-market strategy. 'We're a PLG company with self-serve onboarding.'

terminology.definition: Product Qualified Lead - user who has demonstrated buying intent through product behavior
terminology.usage_context: Lead qualification. 'PQLs convert 5x better than MQLs for us.'

terminology.definition: AI systems that can take autonomous actions rather than just providing recommendations
terminology.usage_context: Current AI trend. 'We're exploring agentic AI for customer support.'

terminology.definition: AI-powered Sales Development Representative - automated outbound prospecting
terminology.usage_context: Sales automation. 'Our AI SDR handles initial outreach and qualification.'

terminology.definition: AI assistant that augments human work rather than replacing it
terminology.usage_context: Product positioning. 'We built an AI copilot for sales teams.'

terminology.definition: Percentage of signups who complete key actions indicating they've experienced product value
terminology.usage_context: Onboarding metric. 'Our Day-7 activation rate is 45%.'

terminology.definition: Service Organization Control 2 - security compliance framework for service providers
terminology.usage_context: Enterprise requirement. 'We need to complete SOC 2 Type II before closing enterprise deals.'

terminology.definition: Requirement that data be stored in specific geographic locations
terminology.usage_context: Compliance requirement. 'European customers require EU data residency.'

common_objections.objection: We don't have budget for this right now
common_objections.underlying_concern: Uncertain about ROI and competing priorities for limited resources
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Quantify ROI with specific metrics relevant to their business. Reference the 3-month ROI expectation and show how value is realized quickly. Offer pilot or smaller initial scope.

common_objections.objection: We're already locked into [competitor] for the next year
common_objections.underlying_concern: Switching costs and disruption seem too high relative to potential benefit
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Acknowledge the commitment and propose running parallel or complementary. Calculate the cost of waiting vs. the value gap. Offer migration support or integration path.

common_objections.objection: I need to get buy-in from the rest of the team first
common_objections.underlying_concern: Fear of championing something that fails or faces internal resistance
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Arm the champion with materials for each stakeholder type. Offer to join internal meetings or provide tailored decks. Create consensus-building assets.

common_objections.objection: We don't have engineering capacity to implement this
common_objections.underlying_concern: Every new tool competes for precious engineering resources
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Demonstrate low-touch or no-code implementation options. Provide realistic implementation timelines from similar customers. Offer implementation support or services.

common_objections.objection: How do we know this will actually work for our use case?
common_objections.underlying_concern: Fear of investing in something that doesn't deliver promised results
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Propose a proof of value with clear success criteria. Share case studies from similar companies. Offer success-based pricing or guarantees if possible.

common_objections.objection: We need SOC 2 compliance and you don't have it yet
common_objections.underlying_concern: Security and compliance are non-negotiable for enterprise customers
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Share SOC 2 timeline if in progress. Provide alternative security documentation. Offer to complete security questionnaire. Consider if they can start with non-sensitive use case.

common_objections.objection: This doesn't integrate with our existing stack
common_objections.underlying_concern: Fragmented tools create friction and reduce adoption
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Map specific integration requirements. Show API capabilities and existing integrations. Offer to build custom integration as part of deal if strategic.

scenario_templates.template: A {{company_size}} SaaS startup just raised {{funding_round}} and the new VP of {{department}} wants to improve {{metric}}. They're currently using {{current_solution}} but experiencing {{pain_point}}.
{{founder_role}} at an early-stage SaaS company is evaluating solutions to address {{pain_point}}. They have {{timeline}} to show results to their board and a budget of {{budget}}.
A Series {{stage}} SaaS company with {{arr}} ARR is facing {{pain_point}}. The buying committee includes {{stakeholders}} and they need to justify the investment based on {{success_metric}}.
{{role}} at a PLG SaaS company is struggling with {{pain_point}}. Their activation rate is {{activation_rate}} and they believe improving {{metric}} could significantly impact revenue.
